prompt:
You are creating a strict inspection checklist for a Quality Control system.
Based on the summary of a normal breakfast_box, generate 5 essential Yes/No questions to detect logical anomalies.

[ Description of breakfast_box ]
1. Components: [Two tangerines, one nectarine (peach), a portion of granola or cereal mix containing banana chips and almonds]
2. Quantities: [Exactly two tangerines; exactly one nectarine]
3. Spatial Arrangement: [The two tangerines are located in specific sections at the left-hand side within their own compartmentalized area inside the box; The single nectarine is placed next to these tangerines but slightly above them due to its larger size compared with an average orange-sized fruit like a tangerine; On the right-hand side, there's another compartment filled with granola/cereal mixture which contains sliced bananas and whole almonds mixed together as part of this breakfast item combination]
4. Visual Appearance: [The overall color scheme includes shades of red from the peaches/tangerines contrasting against the beige tones of the cereals/banana/almond mix]

[ Normal Constraints for breakfast_box ]
- The breakfast box always contains exactly two tangerines and one nectarine that are always located on the left-hand side of the box.
- The ratio and relative position of the cereals and the mix of banana chips and almonds on the right-hand side are fixed.

Using ONLY the constraints listed in [ Normal Constraints for breakfast_box ] and [ Description of breakfast_box ], create exactly 5 simple and important YES/NO questions to determine whether the breakfast_box in the image is normal or abnormal. Ensure the questions are only based on visible characteristics, excluding any aspects that cannot be determined from the image. Also, simplify any difficult terms into easy-to-understand questions.
STRICT RULES:
- Must be answered with a simple "Yes" or "No".
- A "Yes" answer MUST mean the image is NORMAL. A "No" answer MUST mean it is an ANOMALY.
- Each question must evaluate exactly ONE logical constraint (e.g., check quantity OR check position, not both).
- Focus strictly on visible, objective characteristics (quantities, colors, specific positions, presence/absence).
- DO NOT use subjective words (e.g., "good", "proper", "normal").
- DO NOT use negative phrasing (e.g., use "Is the box full?" instead of "Is the box not empty?").

Output ONLY the questions, numbered 1 to 5. Do not add any introductory or concluding text.
(Q1) :
(Q2) :
(Q3) :
(Q4) :
(Q5) :
response:
(Q1) Does the breakfast box contain exactly two tangerines?
(Q2) Is there one nectarine located on the left-hand side of the box, slightly above and next to the tangerines due to its larger size compared with an average orange-sized fruit like a tangerine?
(Q3) Are all three fruits (two tangerines and one nectarine) located in their own compartmentalized area inside the box or are they mixed together without clear separation?
(Q4) Is the granola/cereal mixture containing sliced bananas and whole almonds placed only on the right-hand side within another compartment filled with this mix?
(Q5) Do the overall color scheme include shades of red from the peaches/tangerines contrasting against beige tones of the cereals/banana/almond mix?
parsed_questions: Does the breakfast box contain exactly two tangerines?
Is there one nectarine located on the left-hand side of the box, slightly above and next to the tangerines due to its larger size compared with an average orange-sized fruit like a tangerine?
Are all three fruits (two tangerines and one nectarine) located in their own compartmentalized area inside the box or are they mixed together without clear separation?
Is the granola/cereal mixture containing sliced bananas and whole almonds placed only on the right-hand side within another compartment filled with this mix?
Do the overall color scheme include shades of red from the peaches/tangerines contrasting against beige tones of the cereals/banana/almond mix?
